title: Meta-learning
summary: A subfield of machine learning that involves learning to learn from other models or tasks. It sits inside Learning Paradigms / Learning to Learn. Start after Q-Learning and V-Learning. It opens into Graph Meta-Learning and Hybrid Meta-Learning.

blocks.title: Compare and Contrast

panels.label: What it is
panels.body: A subfield of machine learning that involves learning to learn from other models or tasks.

panels.label: What it is not
panels.body: It is not just probabilistic meta-learning and Meta-Learning with GNNs; the graph shows how it sits inside Learning Paradigms.

panels.label: Common confusion
panels.body: Ask how it differs from nearby concepts and what changes when the term is applied. Compare it against probabilistic meta-learning and Meta-Learning with GNNs so the boundary stays visible instead of collapsing into a synonym pile.

panels.label: Best next step
panels.body: Review Q-Learning and V-Learning first, then move toward Graph Meta-Learning and Hybrid Meta-Learning.

blocks.takeaway: The comparison view keeps Meta-learning honest by showing the nearest neighbors, the real boundary, and the next study move.
